nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Hjalmer, Nels, Thorval.

English (Lancashire, Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Cambridgeshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Warwickshire, named with Middle English toft(e) (Old Norse topt, Old Danish toft), denoting a plot of land on which a house stands or formerly stood. The surname may also be topographic, for someone who lived by or on a toft.
Scandinavian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads or placenames derived from Old Norse topt ‘curtilage, site, homestead’.

variants.name: Tufts
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Tofts, itself a variant of Toft with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Tuft
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, from Old Norse tupt ‘site, lot’.
Possibly an altered form of South German Duft, a topographic name meaning ‘swamp, moor’.

variants.name: Taft
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Toft  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial or metonymic occupational name from German Taft ‘taffeta’.

History: Robert Taft (born c. 1640), lived in Braintree, MA, and subsequently Mendon, MA. — Alphonso Taft (1810–91), jurist and politician born in Townshend, VT, was the father of William Howard Taft (1857–1930), 27th president of the US and chief justice of the US Supreme Court.

variants.name: Tofte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Hjalmer, Iver, Steinar, Thora.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes de Toft was born in 1222, in Plumley, Cheshire, England as the daughter of William Toft and Joan de Lobstock. She married William of Carrington in 1258, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1301, in Carrington, Cheshire, England, at the age of 79.

Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1301, à Carrington, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Toft(은)는 1222년 영국 체실르 플럼리에서 William Toft(와)과 Joan de Lobstock의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1258년 영국 체실르에서 William of Carrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1301년 영국 체실르 카링턴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes de Toft родилась в 1222 г. в местности плумли, чешир, Англия. Её родителей звали William Toft и Joan de Lobstock. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William of Carrington в 1258 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1301 г. в местности каррингтон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 79 лет.
